title: AUTOFILL INCSISTANT WEBSITE TO WEBSITE
content:
Cannot figure out why auto-fill will work with some websites (FedEx), or USPS when I'm scheduling a pickup.

But then at other times, even on USPS, when I attempt to sign up for tracking updates, I get no response to the NAME or EMAIL ADDRESS boxes, and I have to fill them in myself.

Can't figure out the inconsistency. And those were just two examples of sites. It happens frequently. Some fill, with just a click, others force me to type in all the same info that's already saved in my auto-fill memory.

Anyone else seeing this inconsistency, and has figured out a fix or what I'm doing wrong?

Thanks

Lenny

title: Sites only working in Chrome.
content:
Hello all 

 Firstly I have been using Firefox for about 2 decades and it has always worked perfectly - you can take a bow. Now for my problem I have been using Firefox for internet banking for many years without any problems - the usual login, password ans secret questions. Just after Xmas my bank updated it's website and suddenly nothing works. I was told that it now only works on Chrome and NOTHING else - not Firefox, Edge or anything else. I am able to log-in but can't see my account balances or perform any other functions - it just jams up to the point that I even have trouble closing Firefox. I use Firefox exclusively because I don't trust Chrome or Google - they are just a massive data collection/sharing operation. I have Chrome Mask installed but it just doesn't make any difference. 
 Is there anything that I can do or add-on to over-ride this problem?

I am running Mint Linux 22.2 with Firefox 151.0.1

title: Is the value of Origin in https://packages.mozilla.org/apt/dists/mozilla/Release guaranteed to be stable?
content:
I have installed the .deb version of Mozilla Firefox on Ubuntu. It was recently replaced by unattended-upgrades with the snap version. I get the impressions that is due to the fact that unattended-upgrades does not consider https://packages.mozilla.org/apt/dists/mozilla/Release as a source when running.

It seems that it would be possible to configure unattended-upgrades to include https://packages.mozilla.org/apt/dists/mozilla/Release.

The current value for "Origin" in https://packages.mozilla.org/apt/dists/mozilla/Release is "namespaces/moz-fx-productdelivery-pr-38b5/repositories/mozilla" (to me pr-number looks like a pull request).

I would like to confirm whether or not this is a stable value for Origin, and if not - can a stable value be allocated?

title: Why are the homepage suggestions feeding me the most random nonsense from my history?
content: On my homepage dismissing suggestion after suggestion of random websites i've visited singular times. It's creepy as hell the way firefox chooses random shit from my history to suggest to me. Remember that wikipedia article you read last week? They'll suggest that page instead of suggesting wikipedia itself? It's not helpful, I'm not navigating the web quicker because of it, if i wanted to find something from my history I'd search through my fucking history, it's creepy firefox's homepage algorithm tries to do that for me. It's annoying that I can click "dismiss" on a page, and it'll just show up at the end of the list! I fucking hate it and I spent way too long dismissing page after page after page until it finally suggested non-creepy links that i could pin so I never get a fUCKING suggestion for a goddamn reddit discussion I read five months ago ever again. It'd be nice if there was just a way to choose the fucking links that go there, or to fucking tell firefox to stop suggestion certain things outright, but who at mozilla actually gives a shit about what the user wants? I saw what y'all did with the mobile app, ya'll hate your userbase and having a functioning product god damn.

title: youtube not working specifically in firefox
content:
Hello!

For few minutes it says "Performing a TLS handshake to youtube.com", then this message appears:

==

Secure Connection Failed

The page you are trying to view cannot be shown because the authenticity of the received data could not be verified. What can you do about it?

The issue is most likely with the website, and there is nothing you can do to resolve it. You can notify the website’s administrator about the problem.

==

What I did so far:

1. Deleted cache and site data for youtube.com. 2. Checked if date and time setting is set to auto and synced it just in case. 3. Tried restarting firefox in safe mode. 4. Tried turning off ocsp stapling. 5. Deleted ALL cache and site data. 6. Tried creating a new profile. 7. Set HTTPS-only mode to "don't enable" and added youtube.com to exceptions

I do use DoH. I can't not use it. YT won't work without it for sure. Provider is google: https://dns.google/dns-query

Everything works fine in Chrome (yes, I use DoH there too).

title: Firefox Crashes(?) When Disconnecting from VPN
content:
Firefox recently seems to be crashing randomly when I disconnect from VPN. At least I think it's crashing. This has maybe started a few weeks ago?

What happens is that when I disconnect from my VPN (ProtonVPN), I will randomly not be able to load any pages. If I go to any website, it will just get stuck loading forever. No timeout. I'm able to visit the browsers own pages like settings, some about: pages like about:performance, about:memory, etc.

Why I'm not sure if it's crashing is because the browser itself is not closing, I have to manually close the browser window. Once I close the window manually and wait a while, the crash reporter pops up. If I close the window manually then re-open it, theres a text prompt that shows up telling me Firefox is still open and if I want to close it to re-open Firefox.

I don't think it's the VPN's issue as when I disconnect from my VPN, applications other than Firefox work and have internet connectivity (a separate browser like Chrome, or discord, etc)

What I have tried:

  • new Firefox profile
  • Using Firefox Nightly
  • Various ProtonVPN settings (but I don't think its related to ProtonVPN)
  • Various Firefox network settings
  • Both Firefox and Firefox Nightly Open, when disconnecting VPN one would stop letting me reach sites but other wouldn't
